top_categories.workout_context: 1211 miles. Your legs will beg to quit after 120. This trains the power output you need from mile 130 onward.
1211 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Race Around Netherlands' demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before May.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 1211 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Long rides with hard efforts mixed in. Simulates what Race Around Netherlands' mixed terrain actually does to you.
When someone attacks on mixed terrain, you have 10 seconds to respond or you're off the back. This builds that response.
Race Around Netherlands' mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 1211 miles.
Tempo is Race Around Netherlands' race pace. The wattage you'll hold for 1211 miles on mixed terrain. Train it until it's automatic.
Race Around Netherlands' mixed terrain rewards steady threshold power. Surging and recovering costs more watts than just holding.
2-minute all-out efforts on mixed terrain. Closing gaps, responding to attacks, sprinting for position. Train the burn.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Race Around Netherlands. Train it until it feels boring.
How long can you hold 105% FTP before you blow? At Race Around Netherlands, that number decides whether you bridge or get dropped.

race_overlay.nutrition: Ultra-distance fueling for 1211 miles: 80–100g carbs/hour from mile 1 — don’t wait until you’re hungry. Practice your exact race-day nutrition in every long training ride. Carry backup calories. 1211 miles burns 8,000–12,000+ calories — you cannot replace them all, but you must try.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, HVLI Extended, and Race Simulation to prepare you for 1211 miles of mixed terrain in Netherlands (loop).
